jokes.text: I haven't been stared at like that since the last time I had sex.
jokes.setup: Larry describing the uncomfortable audience reaction to his monologue
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his sexual performance, implying both are disasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders self-deprecation - reveals character insecurity while making audience complicit in his humiliation. Well-crafted misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I haven't been stared at like that since the last time I had sex.

jokes.text: When I find Phil, I will introduce his ass to my good friend Mr. Florsheim.
jokes.setup: Artie threatening Phil for writing a bad monologue
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic threat using shoe brand name
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - old-school threat with specific brand reference. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: When I find Phil, I will introduce his ass to my good friend Mr. Florsheim.

jokes.text: Hello. I'd shake hands with you, but you were touching Phil's feet.
jokes.setup: Standard introduction moment
jokes.punchline: Larry's neurotic germaphobia creates social awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry Sanders neurosis - making normal social interaction uncomfortable through his hang-ups.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hello. I'd shake hands with you, but you were touching Phil's feet.

jokes.text: A week... and a half.
jokes.setup: Artie asking how long Phil's been seeing Jessica, after establishing the show's been bad for a week and a half
jokes.punchline: Phil's timing revelation connecting his relationship to the show's decline
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing beat - the pause and delivery make the connection clear without spelling it out. Economy of language with maximum impact.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: A week... and a half.

jokes.text: it was early conan.
jokes.setup: Larry listing ways the monologue was terrible
jokes.punchline: Ultimate insult in late-night TV world - comparing to young Conan
jokes.explanation: Industry-insider humor that works on multiple levels - competitive late-night world and specific era reference that dates the show perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: it was early conan.

jokes.text: it's gonna be stale by tomorrow afternoon.
jokes.setup: Larry demanding Phil start writing tomorrow's monologue immediately
jokes.punchline: Phil's ridiculous concern about comedy having an expiration date
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about the comedy writing process, though not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: it's gonna be stale by tomorrow afternoon.

jokes.text: Yeah, the pussy cult. Whenever I'm getting pussy, I'm not funny.
jokes.setup: Larry asking if Phil joined a cult
jokes.punchline: Artie's crude but accurate diagnosis of Phil's problem
jokes.explanation: Pure Artie - crude wisdom delivered with matter-of-fact confidence. Character-perfect and establishes the episode's central theme.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yeah, the pussy cult. Whenever I'm getting pussy, I'm not funny.

jokes.text: Well, you're never funny.
jokes.setup: Phil saying he's not funny when getting pussy
jokes.punchline: Artie's brutal correction
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed Artie putdown - three words that destroy Phil's entire premise. Maximum economy with brutal efficiency.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, you're never funny.

jokes.text: No one ever died there when it was chasens.
jokes.setup: Phil mentioning Jessica's band is playing at the Viper Room
jokes.punchline: Larry's reference to River Phoenix's death and the venue's history
jokes.explanation: Dark Hollywood history reference that only Larry would make - morbid but witty observation about venue transformation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No one ever died there when it was chasens.

jokes.text: I don't have a natural curiosity about people.
jokes.setup: Larry praising Hank's interview notes
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission of his fundamental personality flaw as a talk show host
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry casually admitting he lacks the basic quality needed for his job. Self-aware but delivered without self-consciousness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't have a natural curiosity about people.

jokes.text: I don't even know which one is harriet. You'll learn their names within the year.
jokes.setup: Larry admitting he doesn't know his staff
jokes.punchline: Hank's optimistic timeline for basic human decency
jokes.explanation: Great character interaction - Hank's patient enabling of Larry's narcissism with gentle sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't even know which one is harriet.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You'll learn their names within the year.

jokes.text: I can only derive a limited amount of personal satisfaction from booking the parrot lady.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining why he wants to leave
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to the mundane celebrity booking reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect behind-the-scenes detail that reveals the unglamorous reality of talk show production.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I can only derive a limited amount of personal satisfaction from booking the parrot lady.

jokes.text: The music stopped! The music stopped! What music?
jokes.setup: Hank playing his hot potato game with Artie
jokes.punchline: Artie's confused response to Hank's imaginary game show music
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank delusion moment - his enthusiasm for his terrible game show idea contrasted with reality.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: The music stopped! The music stopped!

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What music?

jokes.text: But if they get a prize when they have it, why would anybody throw it away?
jokes.setup: Hank explaining his hot potato game twist
jokes.punchline: Artie pointing out the obvious logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man work from Artie, exposing Hank's flawed logic with simple question.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: But if they get a prize when they have it, why would anybody throw it away?

jokes.text: You know Larry only does the jerk-off jokes that he writes.
jokes.setup: Phil offering a masturbation joke for Larry
jokes.punchline: Artie explaining Larry's ego about his masturbation material
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - Larry's ego extends even to his masturbation jokes. Reveals character through specific detail.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know Larry only does the jerk-off jokes that he writes.

jokes.text: she tells me I make her sick.
jokes.setup: Phil describing Jessica breaking up with him after he complimented her band
jokes.punchline: The brutal, unexplained rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor - the inexplicable nature of romantic rejection.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: she tells me I make her sick.

jokes.text: These gophers are also suspected of kidnapping farmers and subjecting them to painful medical probes.
jokes.setup: Joke about crop circles being caused by gophers instead of aliens
jokes.punchline: Escalating the absurdity by giving gophers alien behavior
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist premise that subverts alien conspiracy theories, though could be tighter.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: These gophers are also suspected of kidnapping farmers and subjecting them to painful medical probes.

jokes.text: You're wearing my favorite cologne... 'hilarity by Larry Sanders'
jokes.setup: Artie greeting Larry
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic fake cologne name combining Larry's ego with his comedy attempts
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie sarcasm - mocking Larry's desperation for laughs through fake product branding.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're wearing my favorite cologne... 'hilarity by Larry Sanders'

jokes.text: I said it's fine. ...and, by the way, I'm a dead man for telling you...
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to explain after Artie said it was fine
jokes.punchline: Artie's acknowledgment that Larry's nervousness will get him in trouble
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Artie's understanding of the office politics and Larry's weakness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I said it's fine. ...and, by the way, I'm a dead man for telling you...

jokes.text: O.j. Simpson. He's doin' great. Sensational.
jokes.setup: Story about OJ getting hired to play himself in reenactment
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan commentary on OJ's post-trial career
jokes.explanation: Perfect mid-90s topical humor - dark but not mean, commenting on the absurdity of OJ's situation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: O.j. Simpson. He's doin' great. Sensational.

jokes.text: and in 6 months, I replaced him. You don't have to worry about that. Ha! That's exactly what I said to Bob Sterling.
jokes.setup: Artie telling Paula about learning from Bob Sterling
jokes.punchline: Revealing he became exactly what he learned from, then immediately doing it to Paula
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - Artie unconsciously reveals his pattern while simultaneously denying it. Perfect dramatic irony.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: and in 6 months, I replaced him. You don't have to worry about that. Ha! That's exactly what I said to Bob Sterling.

jokes.text: pain equals funny.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Phil's jokes are suddenly good after being dumped
jokes.punchline: The episode title delivered as industry wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect distillation of comedy theory into three words. Both the show's theme and industry truth delivered by the perfect character.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: pain equals funny.

jokes.text: Well, I hope our whole staff gets dumped. Maybe we'd win an emmy now and then.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining pain makes Phil funny
jokes.punchline: Larry's willingness to sacrifice his staff's happiness for awards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders narcissism - immediately making it about his own success and willing to cause others pain.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, I hope our whole staff gets dumped. Maybe we'd win an emmy now and then.

jokes.text: Better than skinny-dipping with jfk in the white house swimming pool?
jokes.setup: Paula saying David Spade has funny stories
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous comparison to impossible historical scenario
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist reference but feels somewhat random. Shows Larry's tendency to exaggerate.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Better than skinny-dipping with jfk in the white house swimming pool?

jokes.text: you know how well I react to gestures of sentimentality.
jokes.setup: Staff celebrating Paula's promotion with a cake
jokes.punchline: Paula's dry acknowledgment of her own emotional walls
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Paula's self-awareness about her defensive nature.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: you know how well I react to gestures of sentimentality.

jokes.text: You've never thanked me before. Well, the jokes were never that good before.
jokes.setup: Phil noting unusual thanks from Artie
jokes.punchline: Artie's brutal honesty about Phil's usual quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - never missing a chance to put someone down while being completely matter-of-fact about it.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: You've never thanked me before.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, the jokes were never that good before.

jokes.text: he looks kind of green to me. Well, I had a bad piece of fish.

jokes.explanation: Typical Larry oversharing - missing the professional point and making it personal.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: he looks kind of green to me.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, I had a bad piece of fish.

jokes.punchline: Specific visual comparison to Chief Wahoo mascot
jokes.explanation: Decent visual callback but not exceptional. Sets up later payoff.

dialogue.line: he's gonna look like the cleveland indians' mascot.

jokes.text: Artie walking out during the lighting discussion
jokes.setup: Technical production discussion getting heated
jokes.punchline: Artie's silent exit rather than deal with the conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy - Artie's avoidance of conflict shown through action rather than words.

jokes.text: Here. Catch. Then the audience, in unison, shouts, 'hey, now, Hank, go fuck yourself.'

jokes.punchline: Phil's hostile audience response as part of the game show format
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Phil's passive-aggressive revenge delivered as professional game show copy. Perfect tone and unexpected hostility.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Here. Catch. Then the audience, in unison, shouts, 'hey, now, Hank, go fuck yourself.'

jokes.text: Phil writes better and faster when he's unhappy, and we don't have tonight's monologue. Do you understand what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Paula talking to Jessica about Phil's relationship
jokes.punchline: Paula essentially asking Jessica to hurt Phil for professional reasons
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - Paula's cold calculation and manipulation disguised as professional necessity. Morally questionable but brilliantly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Phil writes better and faster when he's unhappy, and we don't have tonight's monologue. Do you understand what I'm saying?

jokes.text: that was your pick-up phrase in the seventies, wasn't it?
jokes.setup: Monologue joke about 'Tickle Me Elmo'
jokes.punchline: Larry's ad-lib connecting it to personal history
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to Larry's past, showing his ability to connect current material to personal experience.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: that was your pick-up phrase in the seventies, wasn't it?

jokes.text: numbers 17 and 56 hate me and won't take my calls.
jokes.setup: Larry not being on Entertainment Weekly's power list
jokes.punchline: Finding pride in being hated by people more powerful than him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders logic - finding validation in rejection. Great character insight delivered as throwaway line.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: numbers 17 and 56 hate me and won't take my calls.

jokes.text: I just can't... what, did I push too hard? Did I do something wrong? Did I not produce the show?
jokes.setup: Larry firing Paula after one day as producer
jokes.punchline: Paula's increasingly desperate questions about her performance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Paula's confusion and desperation contrasted with Larry's inability to explain himself.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I just can't... what, did I push too hard? Did I do something wrong? Did I not produce the show?

jokes.text: I'm not a happy man. I know that I hide it pretty well,
jokes.setup: Larry opening up to Paula about his feelings
jokes.punchline: Larry thinking he hides his obvious misery well
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Larry's complete lack of self-awareness about how obvious his unhappiness is to everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm not a happy man. I know that I hide it pretty well,

jokes.text: I'm not your type. My breasts are real.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Paula out after firing her
jokes.punchline: Paula's cutting assessment of Larry's preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect character assassination delivered with surgical precision. Cuts to core of Larry's Hollywood shallowness in five words.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I'm not your type. My breasts are real.

jokes.text: You know, I could get used to that.
jokes.setup: Paula saying her breasts are real
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate pivot to considering this a selling point
jokes.explanation: Good Larry response - completely missing the insult and focusing on his own adaptation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know, I could get used to that.

jokes.text: The cleveland indians' mascot, Chief wahoo.
jokes.setup: Larry describing how bad he looked on the show
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier mascot reference, now confirmed

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The cleveland indians' mascot, Chief wahoo.

jokes.text: Damn it, it was excellent. Except for your tie.
jokes.setup: Larry saying the show was horrible without Artie
jokes.punchline: Artie's admission the show was great, then petty criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - deflating Larry's ego while maintaining his own authority through petty fashion criticism.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Damn it, it was excellent. Except for your tie.

jokes.text: You shouldn't wear stripes, Sonny. They strobe and make your face look puffy.
jokes.setup: Artie complimenting the show
jokes.punchline: But still criticizing Larry's tie choice
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the tie subplot that shows Artie still caring about production details despite being hurt.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You shouldn't wear stripes, Sonny. They strobe and make your face look puffy.

jokes.text: I also got 5 porno channels. I'll have a beer.
jokes.setup: Artie describing his satellite TV setup
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate decision to stay based on porn availability
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's priorities and male bonding through shared interests.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I also got 5 porno channels.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'll have a beer.

jokes.text: but what I didn't tell you is he was an absolute asshole.
jokes.setup: Artie continuing his Bob Sterling story after saying they discussed it
jokes.punchline: Revealing the important detail he left out
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds important context to earlier story, showing Artie's self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: but what I didn't tell you is he was an absolute asshole.

jokes.text: That's what I was when Larry first saw me.
jokes.setup: Artie describing Paula as young, bright, and pretty
jokes.punchline: Artie's nostalgic recognition of his former self
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Artie's self-awareness about aging and his past appeal.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's what I was when Larry first saw me.

jokes.text: She said you're a really sweet guy. Oh, she's sweet, too.
jokes.setup: Artie saying Paula saw a sweet side of Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's oblivious response missing the implication
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Larry's inability to read subtext or recognize his own behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: She said you're a really sweet guy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, she's sweet, too.

jokes.text: We have no monologue.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing Paula is staying after he confessed his feelings
jokes.punchline: The immediate professional crisis that results from his romantic mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode ending - all the romantic and professional threads collide in Larry's realization of his predicament.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: We have no monologue.
